channel.title: Alsat.Asia — Central Asia Financial Intelligence
channel.description: Independent financial research and market analysis from Central Asia. Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, emerging markets, frontier markets.

item.title: Kazakhstan's $129.5 Billion Reserve: Why This Central Asian Economy Is Quietly Becoming a Frontier Market Powerhouse
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: While most Western investors focus on Southeast Asia and Eastern Europe, Kazakhstan has been building one of the most resilient emerging market balance sheets in the world.

item.title: Almaty Real Estate vs. Dubai vs. New York: A Price-Per-Square-Meter Analysis
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Premium apartments in Almaty cost $1,500–$2,500 per square meter. Dubai: $4,000–$8,000. New York: $12,000–$20,000.

item.title: The Middle Corridor: Why Kazakhstan's 13 Transit Routes Are Reshaping Global Trade
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: With 13 international transit corridors crossing its territory, Kazakhstan is positioning itself as the critical land bridge between China, Europe, and the Middle East.

item.title: Uranium, Lithium, and Rare Earths: Kazakhstan's Critical Mineral Wealth Explained
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Kazakhstan produces 43% of the world's uranium. It holds significant lithium, chromium, manganese, and rare earth deposits.

item.title: Why Kyrgyzstan May Be the Cheapest Place in Central Asia to Start a Business in 2026
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Minimal bureaucracy, low registration costs, a growing fintech scene, and a strategic location between China and Central Asia.

item.title: KASE vs. NYSE: An Honest Look at Kazakhstan's Stock Exchange for Foreign Investors
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: The Kazakhstan Stock Exchange has fewer than 150 listed securities. But for frontier market investors, the question is whether the inefficiency is the opportunity.

item.title: Kazakhstan's Agricultural Frontier: 22 Million Hectares of Arable Land
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Kazakhstan has 22 million hectares of arable land — more than France and Germany combined. The efficiency gap is enormous.

item.title: Kazakhstan's Digital Economy: From E-Government to AI
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Kazakhstan has one of the most digitized government service systems in the post-Soviet space. Its tech sector is growing rapidly.

item.title: From Oil to Solar: Kazakhstan's Renewable Energy Transition
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: A country known for oil and uranium is building solar farms, wind parks, and nuclear plants simultaneously.

item.title: Kazakhstan's Multi-Vector Foreign Policy: How a Nation Between Russia and China Stays Independent
item.dc:creator: Alsat.Asia
item.description: Kazakhstan borders Russia to the north and China to the east, yet has deepened ties with the United States, EU, and Gulf states simultaneously.
